one_time_description: Valuation loss on investment in Machine Works Industrial Ltd. which filed for bankruptcy and was written off
economic_notes: Nation operates Nakota Junction Convenience Store Inc. (100% owned, accounted for by modified equity method). Nakoda Developments LP and Nakoda Developments Corporation were consolidated during the year as they could no longer sustain operations without continued assistance from the Nation. Keseechiwan Holdings LP 8.3% interest held as portfolio investment. Venture opportunities revenue sharing of $1,118,505. COVID-19 funds of $1,100,000 transferred from trust funds.
notes: Fiscal year is 2020, not 2024 as template default. Nation is located in Saskatchewan. Nakoda Developments LP and Nakoda Developments Corporation were brought into consolidation during the year (previously equity method). Significant deferred revenue increase of ~$9M primarily due to ISC Water Treatment Plant Construction funding of $8.6M deferred. Funds held in trust of $14.6M (Ottawa Trust Fund) are financial assets. Large ISC 1905 Surrender Claim loan of $2.6M. COVID-19 funds noted. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $13,579,727 + FNIHB $2,470,391 + CMHC $199,662 + Contributions $1,500,504 = $17,749,504. OSR = $24,429,142 - $17,749,504 = $6,679,638. Audit report signed April 9, 2021, over a year after fiscal year end.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
